task.title: Disabling DNS processing in the NetworkManager configuration

context.p: By default, NetworkManager manages DNS settings in the /etc/resolv.conf file, and you can configure the order of DNS servers. Alternatively, you can disable DNS processing in NetworkManager if you prefer to manually configure DNS settings in /etc/resolv.conf.

step.cmd: As the root user, create the /etc/NetworkManager/conf.d/90-dns-none.conf file with the following content by using a text editor:

note.p: After you reload the service, NetworkManager no longer updates the /etc/resolv.conf file. However, the last contents of the file are preserved.

step.cmd: Optional: Remove the Generated by NetworkManager comment from /etc/resolv.conf to avoid confusion.

li.p: Edit the /etc/resolv.conf file and manually update the configuration.
If you successfully disabled DNS processing, NetworkManager did not override the manually configured settings.
Display the NetworkManager configuration to ensure that no other configuration file with a higher priority overrode the setting:

p.xref: Configuring the order of DNS servers using NetworkManager
